summary: Set 071 Session 2 of 3 -- severity-anchored blocking classifier + re-verify loop discipline. Ran on claude-opus-4-8 (operator-launched; S1's routed rec was sonnet-4-6/medium, operator overrode to opus). OPERATOR DECISION POINT (verdict grammar): routed a cross-provider consult (gpt-5-4 + gemini-pro independently, then a fresh-opus synthesis) per operator request -- UNANIMOUS BINARY (VERIFIED/ISSUES_FOUND); the fresh-Claude refinement (make is_blocking_verdict a first-class, fully-tested, DOCUMENTED contract artifact; bare verdict token is NOT sufficient to infer blocking) was adopted. Raw: s2-grammar-consult-{gpt,gemini,claude-synthesis}.md. DELIVERED: (1) ai_router/verification.py -- is_blocking_verdict(verdict,issues) (SEVERITY-DERIVED not token-derived: any Critical/Major or unknown/missing-severity finding blocks regardless of token; all-Minor -> non-blocking; no-findings -> VERIFIED non-blocking, non-VERIFIED blocking; anti-laundering 'when in doubt escalate') + classify_blocking/BlockingClassification (blocking/nit partition + log reason) + reconcile_issue_ledger/LedgerReconciliation (stable-id RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED + resurrection flag for the no-resurrection-under-new-wording rule) + parse_nits (additive, observability-only NITS reader -- nits NEVER enter the issues list, an S1 invariant) + SURFACE NOTE documenting the classifier is surface-agnostic (push via parse_verification_response, pull via pull_verifier.Finding.severity). Plus LOAD-BEARING parse_verification_response robustness fixes surfaced by the churn fixture + verification: the 'ISSUES FOUND' header self-match (spurious severity-less finding), the '**Severity:** Minor' regex that could not read markdown-bold severity (-> Minor read as unknown -> blocking = the churn this set kills), the canonical underscored 'ISSUES_FOUND' header not stripped (L-069-1 class-completion: '_' is not whitespace), a 'VERDICT:'-prefixed verdict falling through to ISSUES_FOUND, and a trailing NITS section bleeding into the last issue's description (now stripped before issue parsing). parse_verification_response's (verdict,issues) contract preserved. (2) ai_router/__init__.py -- exported the new symbols + WIRED the predicate into the one programmatic verification site: VerificationResult gained blocking (=is_blocking_verdict) + nits (=parse_nits) fields (defaulted, backward-compatible), populated in _run_verification so the re-verify loop reads result.verification.blocking instead of the bare token. (3) docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md -- new Step-6 subsection 'Materiality and the re-verify loop discipline (Set 071)' (severity-anchored blocking via is_blocking_verdict NOT the bare token; Minor-only = effectively VERIFIED, opens no round; anti-laundering; cross-round issue ledger, no resurrection under new wording; surface-agnostic; 1-2/3+ bound unchanged -- only narrows what counts as a round-justifying finding; cites L-069-2) + wired Step-7 ISSUES_FOUND branch + the max-2-retries item + the Mode-B bounded-round item to it (L-065-1 echo discipline). (4) ai_router/tests/test_blocking_classifier.py (new) -- is_blocking_verdict matrix, classify_blocking partition, the VERBATIM pytest-vs-`python -m pytest -v` churn (3 rounds, end-to-end through parse_verification_response -> Minor-only -> NON-blocking), verdict-grammar variants (VERDICT:-prefixed VERIFIED, underscored ISSUES_FOUND Minor/Major), severity-derived-not-token (Major under mislabeled VERIFIED blocks at the predicate level), push-parser-trusts-VERIFIED-token (no false positive from prose discussing severity), NITS-no-bleed, parse_nits, surface-agnostic over pull_verifier.Finding, VerificationResult wiring, and the reconcile_issue_ledger tests. NO release (PyPI bump is S3). DESIGN: binary grammar preserved (no third VERIFIED_WITH_NITS token); blocking-ness is a derived predicate. SUITE: full ai_router GREEN 2165 passed / 5 skipped (S1 baseline 2126/5; the 5 skips are the Set 069 S4 real-podman-on-Windows by-design skips). VERIFICATION (cross-provider, gpt-5-4, routed_gate REQUIRED via blast-radius/multi-module/breadth; up-front conventions block per L-064-10; strong devil's-advocate framing): SIX rounds. R1 Major (REAL, ADOPTED): the header self-match fix was incomplete -- underscored 'ISSUES_FOUND' not stripped + 'VERDICT: VERIFIED' misparsed (L-069-1 class-completion) -> fixed + regressions. R2 Major (FALSE POSITIVE, ADJUDICATED + hardened): claimed parse_verification_response must learn the path-aware 'Findings' grammar -- architecturally wrong (the pull surface produces structured pull_verifier.Finding objects with native severity and never routes through this parser); answered constructively with a surface-agnostic cross-surface test + a SURFACE NOTE rather than wrongly teaching the push parser the pull grammar. R3 Major (REAL, ADOPTED): is_blocking_verdict short-circuited on the VERIFIED token while the doc said 'NOT the bare token' -> made it severity-DERIVED (a Major present blocks regardless of token) + symmetric classify_blocking + additive parse_nits for the NITS read side. R4 Major + Nit (REAL, ADOPTED then PARTLY REVERTED): pushed the anti-laundering guarantee end-to-end on the push surface -> I over-reached with a whole-response 'Severity: Major' substring scan. R5 Major x2: Issue 1 caught that the R4 substring scan REINTRODUCED the exact false-positive churn the set kills (a clean VERIFIED that merely discusses severity in prose) -> REVERTED to trusting the VERIFIED token on the push surface (the safe, pre-existing, churn-free behavior; anti-laundering safety net lives in is_blocking_verdict for the pull surface + direct callers); Issue 2 (the classifier had no programmatic consumer) -> WIRED blocking/nits onto VerificationResult. R6 Major: re-flagged the token-trust-vs-severity-derivation tension -- directly CONFLICTING with R5's Major (no free lunch via the loose issue_pattern, which matches 'issue' inside prose). This was a GENUINE orchestrator-vs-verifier DESIGN SPLIT on the verification surface itself, at round 6 -> ESCALATED to the operator per the bounded-round '3+ human' rule + 'verifiers flag, humans adjudicate'. OPERATOR ADJUDICATED: TRUST the VERIFIED token (recorded via record_adjudication: genuine-split / accept-dismissal). Adjudicated session verdict: VERIFIED. DOGFOOD NOTE: this session's own verification loop was a live demonstration of the set's discipline -- R1/R3/R4 were real Critical/Major correctness defects (justified rounds, not nit churn), R2 a correctly-adjudicated false positive, and a genuine design split escalated to the human at the round bound rather than churning. All 6 raw verifier outputs saved as s2-verification[-round-N].md (never edited); harnesses run_s2_grammar_consult.py + run_s2_verification.py.

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s2-next-orchestrator.md, gemini-pro) recommends claude / claude-opus-4-8 / medium for S3. S3 ships an IRREVERSIBLE PyPI release and authors durable guidance (lesson L-071-1 + the verification-surface-strategy synthesis), which favor a Tier-3 model's reliability and continuity with S1/S2's perspective; effort medium because the work is well-specified runbook execution + synthesis, not oracle-free divergent architecture, so high effort risks over-engineering on a pre-defined plan. S3 also runs the required multi-provider dogfood (pathAwareCritique: required) and routes the next-SET recommendation.
